name: Cinnamon Pecan Braid
servings: serves 4 | prep. time 30 minutes | cooking time 20 minutes
intro: Pecans braids are very hard to resist when they come out of the oven luring you in with their fresh aroma and delicate cinnamon-pecan filling.
method: Dissolve the yeast in a bowl with ¼ cup of warm water.
Add the eggs and remaining water. Mix well.
In another bowl, combine the flour, sugar, and salt. Cut in the butter until crumbly.
Beat the flour mixture into the yeast mixture (do not knead). Cover and refrigerate overnight.
For the filling, cream the butter and brown sugar in a small bowl. Stir in the pecans and cinnamon; set aside.

Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and divide it into 4 portions. Roll each into a 12×9 rectangle on a greased baking sheet. Spread the filling lengthwise down the center third of each rectangle.
On each long side, cut ¾-inch-wide strips toward the center to within ½ inch of the filling.
Starting at one end, fold the strips alternately at an angle across the filling. Pinch the ends to seal and tuck under. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour (dough will not double).
Bake at 350°F (177°C) for 18–20 minutes or until golden brown. Let cool slightly before removing from baking sheet to wire rack.
Combine the glaze ingredients EXCEPT for the pecans.
Place a pecan half in the middle of each and drizzle generously over the cooled braids.
